name: Duchen, Marc
resolved_employer: City of Austin — Council Member, District 10 (sworn in Jan 6, 2025); previously founder of a civic-engagement data/technology consulting business
evidence: Marc Duchen is the sitting Austin City Council Member for District 10, which covers Northwest Austin including the donor's 78759 zip; his 2024 campaign address was 4711 Spicewood Springs Rd, Austin 78759. Donation pattern strongly corroborates: Travis County Commissioner Brigid Shea publicly endorsed Duchen in his 2024 District 10 race, calling him a 'proven progressive leader,' and this donor's only recipient is Shea (first gift 2025-06-30). Duchen is a UT Austin government grad with an MBA, a former Texas Democratic Party research director and Democratic campaign manager, and a leading neighborhood/land-use activist — board member of Community Not Commodity and spokesman for the plaintiffs in the litigation that killed CodeNEXT and later HOME-era code changes. FEC PAC search surfaced no ideological PAC giving (record shows 5 federal donations, fully Democratic lean); no Texas Ethics Commission lobbyist registration found under the Duchen surname.

name: Ramirez, Jill
resolved_employer: Latino HealthCare Forum (LHCF), Austin — President/CEO and co-founder
evidence: Confirmed as Jill Ramirez, President/CEO and co-founder of the Latino HealthCare Forum (LHCF, 6601 Felix Ave, Austin TX 78741) — the employer string 'LHCF' plus occupation 'CEO' match exactly. Holds master's degrees from UT Austin; former AISD bilingual speech pathologist and educational consultant; LHCF was the only federal ACA Navigator in Central Texas under her leadership. NOTE: the record's zip 79745 is Kermit, TX (Winkler County) and conflicts with the 'Austin, TX' location — almost certainly a data-entry error for an Austin 787xx zip; identity is nonetheless confirmed by the employer/occupation match. SEPARATE CAUTION: the record's fec_partisan_lean 0.49 / 87 federal donations should not be attributed to her without verification — 'Jill Ramirez' is a common name and FEC.gov and OpenSecrets both blocked direct retrieval, so the FEC PAC search was run via web search only and returned no PAC records tied to this individual. No Texas Ethics Commission lobbyist registration found. Also of public record: DOJ announced in 2020 that a federal grand jury in Austin indicted her on one count of conspiracy, one count of falsification of records with intent to obstruct, and one count of false statements, over a September 2015 agreement to pay 10% of the nonprofit's federal grant proceeds to Frank Rodriguez, LHCF's co-founder and then a senior policy advisor to a City of Austin official; DOJ said Rodriguez pleaded guilty. These are charges, not convictions, and the disposition of her case is not established by the sources reviewed — recorded here as an unresolved allegation, and no affiliation is derived from it.

affiliations.snippet: Jill Ramirez is the CEO and one of the founders of the Latino HealthCare Forum; under her guidance LHCF served as the only Federal Navigator for the Affordable Care Act in Central Texas and instituted the Rundberg Innovation Zone place-based initiative.

name: Castaneda, Orlando

evidence: Orlando Castaneda is listed by name and rank as a Corporal on the official Travis County Constable Precinct 4 command staff & deputies roster, matching the record's occupation 'Deputy' and employer 'Travis County Constable's Office' and the Austin 78753 address. Donation pattern strongly corroborates: recipient George Morales III was the elected Constable of Precinct 4 from 2017 to 2025 — i.e., Castaneda's own office head — and Morales went on to run for Travis County Commissioner Precinct 4; the first gift date of 2025-03-25 aligns with that campaign. This is law-enforcement employment, and the donation is from an employee to their own department head's campaign. No FEC individual/PAC contribution records surfaced for this name (FEC.gov and OpenSecrets blocked direct retrieval), and no Texas Ethics Commission lobbyist registration was found. No deputies' association or law-enforcement PAC affiliation is recorded: searches for a documented tie between this specific person and the Travis County Sheriff's Officers' Association, TCSLEA, or any peace-officer PAC returned nothing naming him, and membership must not be inferred from employment alone.
